category_sources: Official ENERGY docs describe it as a Go framework based on LCL and CEF for building cross-platform desktop apps: https://energye.github.io/
source_facts.description says it initializes Energy development environment tools.
display_name_sources: Official homepage heading uses ENERGY: https://energye.github.io/
docs_sources: Official site navigation includes a Documentation section at https://energye.github.io/document/ and getting-started tutorial at https://energye.github.io/course/getting-started.
repo_sources: source_facts.repo already has https://github.com/energye/energy; per instruction, repo returned as null.
tags_sources: Official homepage says ENERGY uses Go with LCL and CEF to build cross-platform desktop apps: https://energye.github.io/
source_facts.description and executables identify a CLI tool.
